task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Incident Template' tiddler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tiddler titled 'Incident Template' is visible in the TiddlyWiki interface, either in edit mode or view mode.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Incident Template' has the correct tag and custom fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'Security Incident' is present on the template tiddler. In the tiddler editor, custom fields 'status' (value: 'Open') and 'severity' (value: 'Triage') are visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Incident Template' body contains the required exact headings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text area of the template contains exactly '# Incident Summary', '# Indicators of Compromise (IoCs)', and '# Remediation Actions'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation and configuration of the '$:/custom/buttons/NewIncident' tiddler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tiddler titled '$:/custom/buttons/NewIncident' is visible, tagged with '$:/tags/PageControls'. It should have fields or content setting the caption to 'New Incident', the description to 'Create a new security incident report', and include a visual icon.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'New Incident' button appears in the TiddlyWiki sidebar Page Controls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new button (with an icon) is visible in the right-hand sidebar under the page controls area (typically next to standard buttons like edit, new tiddler, save, etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify the button successfully triggers the creation of a 'New Security Incident' tiddler based on the template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A newly created or draft tiddler titled 'New Security Incident' is visible, pre-populated with the '# Incident Summary', '# Indicators of Compromise (IoCs)', and '# Remediation Actions' headings, and tagged with 'Security Incident'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the TiddlyWiki graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the TiddlyWiki web interface in a browser being used to create and edit tiddlers, rather than terminal commands or direct file manipulation.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual TiddlyWiki application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard TiddlyWiki layout, editor fields, sidebar, and page controls are visible and actively being used.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually configured the tiddlers rather than bypassing the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: A progression of screenshots showing the agent opening new tiddlers, typing titles, adding tags, adding custom fields, and saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The template tiddler must be exactly titled 'Incident Template' and tagged with 'Security Incident'. The button tiddler must be exactly titled '$:/custom/buttons/NewIncident' and tagged with '$:/tags/PageControls'.
